=== PAGE: https://icfcc.org/visa.html ===
Link: Visa
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Visa Information
With regards to visa inquiries, you can contact âForeign Residents Support Center (FRESC) MOFA Visa Informationâ or âthe Japan Visa Information Hotlineâ listed on the website of
the Embassy or Consulate General of Japan
. Due to the complex nature of visa requirements, we are UNABLE to respond to visa-related inquiries made through this website at the moment. We advise you to contact the Consular Section of the Embassy or Consulate General of Japan nearest you for more information and advice.

If you are a foreign national, please click the links below for specific information related to respective subjects. Please get more details here
https://www.mofa.go.jp/j_info/visit/visa/index.html
Invitation Letters
1. The invitation letter will be issued after the successful registration.
2. Your name must be listed exactly as it appears on your passport. Any differences between the name on your passport and the name on your invitation letter or other documentation could lead to a delay and/or denial of your visa.
3. Please note ICFCC Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference are not authorized to assist with the VISA process beyond providing the Notification of Invitation Letter issued by 
						ICFCC Conferences and conference Committee Board. Should your application be denied, 
						ICFCC Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference cannot change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, nor will 
						ICFCC Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference engage in discussion or correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot be refunded when the VISA application of individual being denied.
4. It takes around one month for the Embassy to process the VISA application, please register as early as you can to make sure you have enough time.

=== PAGE: https://icfcc.org/session.html ===
Link: Special Sessions
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Call for Special Sessions
In addition to the technical sessions, keynote addresses 
						etc., ICFCC 202
6
will held a series of half day special 
						sessions. ICFCC welcome the proposal of special 
						sessions.
These special sessions should highlight current topics 
						related to computer and communication, and should include regular 
						papers, invited presentations, and panels that encourage 
						the participation of attendees in active discussion. We 
						intend to organize a fruitful exchange of opinions and 
						ideas between specialists in power engineering, as well 
						as encourage and facilitate interdisciplinary 
						communication amongst university researches and industry 
						professionals involved in power engineering.
Special Session Proposal
Special-session proposals should be submitted by the 
						prospective organizer(s) who will commit to promoting 
						and handling the review process of their special session 
						as Chairs or Co-Chairs of the event. Proposals should 
						include the following information:
â¢ Title of the Special Session
â¢ General information of organizers (mail address of main 
						contact person, biodata)
â¢ Brief description of special session
â¢ Scope and topics of of the special session
â¢ Names of potential participants, such as program 
						committee members and invited speakers
â¢ Please send your proposal to
icfcc@sciei.org
.
Key Day of Special Session Proposal
â¢ Proposal Deadline: 
						April 10, 202
6
â¢ Notification Deadline: April 15, 202
6
â¢ Complete set: April 20, 202
6

=== PAGE: https://icfcc.org/submit.html ===
Link: Submit Now
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Submission Type
â¢ Full Paper (Presentation and Publication)
(
Template Download for Proceedings
) (
Template Download 
						for Journal
)
Accepted full papers will be invited to give 
						presentation at the conference. It will be published in 
						the conference proceedings.
â¢ Abstract (Presentation only)
(
Template Download
)
Accepted abstract will be invited to give presentation 
						at the conference. The presentation will not be 
						published and included in program only.
â¢ Listeners & Groups & Sponsors
Interested Listeners & Groups & Sponsors, please contact 
						with
icfcc@sciei.org
for details.
Submission Method
The online submission system is opened here:
https://www.zmeeting.org/submission/icfcc2026
You need an account, if you don't have, you can register 
						first.
Submission Guidelines
â¢ Abstracts or full papers will be accepted in English 
						only.
â¢ An international panel of reviewers will review all 
						abstracts or papers anonymously.
â¢ Prospective authors are invited to submit original, 
						high quality papers which is original and has not been 
						submitted and published elsewhere.
â¢ There is no fee for submitting an abstract.
â¢ The title must not exceed 125 characters in length; 
						this includes spaces.
â¢ Manuscripts should be at least 5 full pages and at 
						most 10 pages.
â¢ For one author, you can submit at most 5 papers.
â¢ The reviewing process of the ICFCC conference aims to 
						provide authors with constructive feedback on their 
						papers, even when a submission is rejected. All 
						submissions will be subjected to double-blind peer 
						reviews, who are expert or have been experiencing in the 
						related field for years. The accepted papers must be 
						revised, taking into consideration the referees' 
						comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the 
						conference proceedings.
Review Process
We encourage submissions from under-represented areas. 
						We recognize that not every paper will necessarily 
						contain new theoretical results or new algorithms, and 
						strongly encourages the submissions that provide new 
						models and analyses that are rigorously validated 
						through implementation and experimentation. We will make 
						every effort to fairly review papers with a systems 
						focus.
Policy on Multiple Submissions
We will not accept any paper which, at the time of 
						submission, is under review, is accepted for 
						publication, or has already been published in another 
						conference or a journal. ICFCC is utterly intolerant of 
						plagiarism. Submitted papers are expected to contain 
						original work executed by the authors with adequate, 
						proper and scholarly citations to the work of others. It 
						is the job of the authors to clearly identify both their 
						own contribution(s) and also published results / 
						techniques on which they depend or build. Reviewers are 
						charged to ensure these standards are met.
Instructions for Accepted Papers
After registration, the final paper submitted designates 
						your paper as "Camera Ready" and it will be available 
						online as part of the conference proceedings. Please 
						note that there will not be an additional opportunity to 
						revise your paper. In preparing for the proceedings, 
						please follow the author guidelines posted above under 
						"Paper Format."

=== PAGE: https://icfcc.org/terms.html ===
Link: Conference Terms
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Refund/Cancellation Policy
If a registrant is unable to attend an event for any 
						reason, they may ask the co-author or someone else for 
						help and do the presentation for the registrant. But the 
						registrant should send e-mail for both conference side 
						and the person who will attend.
Written requests for cancellations must be sent to the 
						Conference Secretary. The time would be counted by the 
						refund requests being sent by registrant.
Three Months before conference date: 30USD handing fee 
						would be charged
Two Months before conference date: Refundment of 70% for 
						the registration fee you paid
One Month before conference date: Refundment of 50% for 
						the registration fee you paid
Less Than One Month before conference date: NO REFUND.
Personal Reason
No refund or compensation on "personal reason" for the 
						reason of no attending to the conference. Such as travel 
						difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial 
						default etc.
Force Majeure
No refund or pay any compensation on "force majeure" for 
						the reason of no attending to the conference.
Circumstances amounting to "force majeure" include any 
						event which we could not, even with all due care, 
						foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, 
						flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, 
						break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, 
						industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, 
						adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual 
						or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all 
						similar situations beyond our control.

=== PAGE: https://icfcc.org/2023.html ===
Link: ICFCC 2023
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
ICFCC 202
3
, March 
						10-12, 2023 | Singapore
Welcome you all to 15th International Conference on Future Computer and Communication (ICFCC 2023), which was 
						held in Singapore during March 10-12, 2023 successfully. 
						The conference was organized by Science and Engineering 
						Institute (SCIEI), National Chung Hsing University, 
						assisted by Chuo University, University of Canberra.
Enjoy the Conference
This conference program was highlighted by 3 Keynote 
						Speakers: Prof. Xudong Jiang delivered keynote speech
Towards Explainable AI: How Deep CNN Solves Problems of 
						ANN
; Prof. Cheng-kuo Lee delivered keynote speech
AI-Enhanced Sensors and Applications
, Prof. Naoyuki Ishimuraand delivered keynote speech
Computational Mathematics and Analytical Methods
, and Prof. Bakhodirzhon Siddikov delivered keynote speech
Teaching Numerical Algorithms with Mathematica and Maple.
The chairman Prof. Po-Liang Liu host the session.
Then, 6 sessions were held and discussed topics within Computational Mathematics and Analytical Methods, Machine Learning and Data Analysis in Control and Management System, Basic Physics and Applied Physics, Mathematical Model and Calculation, etc
Winners of Best Oral Presentation

Biography:
Zuqing Zhu 
						received his Ph.D. degree from the Department of 
						Electrical and Computer Engineering, University of 
						California, Davis, in 2007. From 2007 to 2011, he worked 
						in the Service Provider Technology Group of Cisco 
						Systems, San Jose, California, as a Senior Engineer. In 
						January 2011, he joined the University of Science and 
						Technology of China, where he currently is a Full 
						Professor in the School of Information Science and 
						Technology. He has published 360+ papers in 
						peer-reviewed journals and conferences. He is the 
						Steering Committee Chair of the IEEE International 
						Conference on High Performance Switching and Routing 
						(HPSR), and was the Chair of the Technical Committee on 
						Optical Networking (ONTC) in IEEE Communications 
						Society. He has received the Best Paper Awards from ICC 
						2013, GLOBECOM 2013, ICNC 2014, ICC 2015, ONDM 2018, and

=== PAGE: https://icfcc.org/presentation.html ===
Link: Presentations
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Presentations and Equipment
All presentation rooms are equipped with a screen, an 
						LCD projector, and a laptop computer installed with 
						PowerPoint software. You will be able to insert your USB 
						flash drive into the computer and double click on your 
						presentation to open it in PowerPoint. We recommend that 
						you bring two copies of your presentation in case of one 
						fails. You may also link your own laptop computer to the 
						projector cable, however if you use your own Mac please 
						ensure you have the requisite connector.
Session Chairs
Session Chairs are asked to introduce themselves and 
						other speakers (briefly), and ensure that the session 
						begins and ends on time, and that the time is divided 
						fairly between the presentations. Each presenter should 
						have about 15 minutes in which to present his or her 
						paper, and respond to any questions.
A Polite Request to All Participants
Participants are requested to arrive in a timely fashion 
						for all addresses, whether to their own, or to those of 
						other presenters. Presenters are reminded that the time 
						slots should be divided fairly and equally between the 
						number of presentations, and that they should not 
						overrun. The session chair is asked to assume this 
						timekeeping role.

Biography:
Zuqing Zhu 
						received his Ph.D. degree from the Department of 
						Electrical and Computer Engineering, University of 
						California, Davis, in 2007. From 2007 to 2011, he worked 
						in the Service Provider Technology Group of Cisco 
						Systems, San Jose, California, as a Senior Engineer. In 
						January 2011, he joined the University of Science and 
						Technology of China, where he currently is a Full 
						Professor in the School of Information Science and 
						Technology. He has published 360+ papers in 
						peer-reviewed journals and conferences. He is the 
						Steering Committee Chair of the IEEE International 
						Conference on High Performance Switching and Routing 
						(HPSR), and was the Chair of the Technical Committee on 
						Optical Networking (ONTC) in IEEE Communications 
						Society. He has received the Best Paper Awards from ICC 
						2013, GLOBECOM 2013, ICNC 2014, ICC 2015, ONDM 2018, and 
						GLOBECOM 2023. He is a Fellow of IEEE.

â¢ Listeners & Groups & Sponsors
Interested Listeners & Groups & Sponsors, please contact

icfcc@sciei.org
for details.
Submission Method
The online submission system is opened here:
https://www.zmeeting.org/submission/icfcc2026
You need an account, if you don't have, you can register 
						first.
Submission Guidelines
â¢ Abstracts or full papers will be accepted in English 
						only.
â¢ An international panel of reviewers will review all 
						abstracts or papers anonymously.
â¢ Prospective authors are invited to submit original, 
						high quality papers which is original and has not been 
						submitted and published elsewhere.
â¢ There is no fee for submitting an abstract.
â¢ The title must not exceed 125 characters in length; 
						this includes spaces.
â¢ Manuscripts should be at least 5 full pages and at 
						most 10 pages.
â¢ For one author, you can submit at most 5 papers.
â¢ The reviewing process of the ICFCC conference aims to 
						provide authors with constructive feedback on their 
						papers, even when a submission is rejected. All 
						submissions will be subjected to double-blind peer 
						reviews, who are expert or have been experiencing in the 
						related field for years. The accepted papers must be 
						revised, taking into consideration the referees' 
						comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the 
						conference proceedings.
Review Process
We encourage submissions from under-represented areas. 
						We recognize that not every paper will necessarily 
						contain new theoretical results or new algorithms, and 
						strongly encourages the submissions that provide new 
						models and analyses that are rigorously validated 
						through implementation and experimentation. We will make 
						every effort to fairly review papers with a systems 
						focus.
Policy on Multiple Submissions
We will not accept any paper which, at the time of 
						submission, is under review, is accepted for 
						publication, or has already been published in another 
						conference or a journal. ICFCC is utterly intolerant of 
						plagiarism. Submitted papers are expected to contain 
						original work executed by the authors with adequate, 
						proper and scholarly citations to the work of others. It 
						is the job of the authors to clearly identify both their 
						own contribution(s) and also published results / 
						techniques on which they depend or build. Reviewers are 
						charged to ensure these standards are met.
Instructions for Accepted Papers
After registration, the final paper submitted designates 
						your paper as "Camera Ready" and it will be available 
						online as part of the conference proceedings. Please 
						note that there will not be an additional opportunity to 
						revise your paper. In preparing for the proceedings, 
						please follow the author guidelines posted above under 
						"Paper Format."

Link: Conference Terms
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Refund/Cancellation Policy
If a registrant is unable to attend an event for any 
						reason, they may ask the co-author or someone else for 
						help and do the presentation for the registrant. But the 
						registrant should send e-mail for both conference side 
						and the person who will attend.
Written requests for cancellations must be sent to the 
						Conference Secretary. The time would be counted by the 
						refund requests being sent by registrant.
Three Months before conference date: 30USD handing fee 
						would be charged
Two Months before conference date: Refundment of 70% for 
						the registration fee you paid
One Month before conference date: Refundment of 50% for 
						the registration fee you paid
Less Than One Month before conference date: NO REFUND.
Personal Reason
No refund or compensation on "personal reason" for the 
						reason of no attending to the conference. Such as travel 
						difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial 
						default etc.
Force Majeure
No refund or pay any compensation on "force majeure" for 
						the reason of no attending to the conference.
Circumstances amounting to "force majeure" include any 
						event which we could not, even with all due care, 
						foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, 
						flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, 
						break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, 
						industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, 
						adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual 
						or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all 
						similar situations beyond our control.

Visa Information

Invitation Letters

=== PAGE: https://icfcc.org/presentation.html ===
Link: Presentations
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Presentations and Equipment
All presentation rooms are equipped with a screen, an 
						LCD projector, and a laptop computer installed with 
						PowerPoint software. You will be able to insert your USB 
						flash drive into the computer and double click on your 
						presentation to open it in PowerPoint. We recommend that 
						you bring two copies of your presentation in case of one 
						fails. You may also link your own laptop computer to the 
						projector cable, however if you use your own Mac please 
						ensure you have the requisite connector.
Session Chairs
Session Chairs are asked to introduce themselves and 
						other speakers (briefly), and ensure that the session 
						begins and ends on time, and that the time is divided 
						fairly between the presentations. Each presenter should 
						have about 15 minutes in which to present his or her 
						paper, and respond to any questions.
A Polite Request to All Participants
Participants are requested to arrive in a timely fashion 
						for all addresses, whether to their own, or to those of 
						other presenters. Presenters are reminded that the time 
						slots should be divided fairly and equally between the 
						number of presentations, and that they should not 
						overrun. The session chair is asked to assume this 
						timekeeping role.

The 17th International Conference on Future Computer and 
						Communication (ICFCC 2025) was held in Jeju Island, South Korea during June 28 to 30, 2024. The conference is 
						sponsored by Science and Engineering Institute (SCIEI), 
						Sensors and Systems Society of Singapore (SSS), 
						supported by Okayama University, University of Ãvora, 
						Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University, and National Taiwan 
						University of Science and Technology.
Enjoy the Conference
ICFCC
2025 is a great chance for sharing the latest insights of academic and industrial research, bringing together interested academics and industry experts. Prof. Zuqing Zhu, University of Science and Technology of China; Prof. Hung-Hsu Tsai, National Chung Hsing 
						University; Prof. Emanuel S. Grant, University of North 
						Dakota make their excellent speeches.;
Then parallel sessions was held until, and main discussed about Design and Development of Image-based Information Systems; Model Design, Algorithm Optimization, and System Evaluation in Advanced Electronics and Information Systems; Modern Information Networks and Security; Modern Power Systems and Energy Engineering; Software and Mobile Information System Design; Data Based Computational Models and Information Management;
Data based image models and detection techniques. Around 80 people join this event, 6 onsite sessions and 3 online sessions were divided. One best presentation will be selected from each session, evaluated from: originality; applicability; technical Merit; qualities of PPT; English. 9 best presenters were selected.

Biography:
Zuqing Zhu 
						received his Ph.D. degree from the Department of 
						Electrical and Computer Engineering, University of 
						California, Davis, in 2007. From 2007 to 2011, he worked 
						in the Service Provider Technology Group of Cisco 
						Systems, San Jose, California, as a Senior Engineer. In 
						January 2011, he joined the University of Science and 
						Technology of China, where he currently is a Full 
						Professor in the School of Information Science and 
						Technology. He has published 360+ papers in 
						peer-reviewed journals and conferences. He is the 
						Steering Committee Chair of the IEEE International 
						Conference on High Performance Switching and Routing 
						(HPSR), and was the Chair of the Technical Committee on 
						Optical Networking (ONTC) in IEEE Communications 
						Society. He has received the Best Paper Awards from ICC 
						2013, GLOBECOM 2013, ICNC 2014, ICC 2015, ONDM 2018, and 
						GLOBECOM 2023. He is a Fellow of IEEE.

Interested Listeners & Groups & Sponsors, please contact

icfcc@sciei.org
for details.
Submission Method
The online submission system is opened here:
https://www.zmeeting.org/submission/icfcc2026
You need an account, if you don't have, you can register 
						first.
Submission Guidelines
â¢ Abstracts or full papers will be accepted in English 
						only.
â¢ An international panel of reviewers will review all 
						abstracts or papers anonymously.
â¢ Prospective authors are invited to submit original, 
						high quality papers which is original and has not been 
						submitted and published elsewhere.
â¢ There is no fee for submitting an abstract.
â¢ The title must not exceed 125 characters in length; 
						this includes spaces.
â¢ Manuscripts should be at least 5 full pages and at 
						most 10 pages.
â¢ For one author, you can submit at most 5 papers.
â¢ The reviewing process of the ICFCC conference aims to 
						provide authors with constructive feedback on their 
						papers, even when a submission is rejected. All 
						submissions will be subjected to double-blind peer 
						reviews, who are expert or have been experiencing in the 
						related field for years. The accepted papers must be 
						revised, taking into consideration the referees' 
						comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the 
						conference proceedings.
Review Process
We encourage submissions from under-represented areas. 
						We recognize that not every paper will necessarily 
						contain new theoretical results or new algorithms, and 
						strongly encourages the submissions that provide new 
						models and analyses that are rigorously validated 
						through implementation and experimentation. We will make 
						every effort to fairly review papers with a systems 
						focus.
Policy on Multiple Submissions
We will not accept any paper which, at the time of 
						submission, is under review, is accepted for 
						publication, or has already been published in another 
						conference or a journal. ICFCC is utterly intolerant of 
						plagiarism. Submitted papers are expected to contain 
						original work executed by the authors with adequate, 
						proper and scholarly citations to the work of others. It 
						is the job of the authors to clearly identify both their 
						own contribution(s) and also published results / 
						techniques on which they depend or build. Reviewers are 
						charged to ensure these standards are met.
Instructions for Accepted Papers
After registration, the final paper submitted designates 
						your paper as "Camera Ready" and it will be available 
						online as part of the conference proceedings. Please 
						note that there will not be an additional opportunity to 
						revise your paper. In preparing for the proceedings, 
						please follow the author guidelines posted above under 
						"Paper Format."

Link: Conference Terms
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Refund/Cancellation Policy
If a registrant is unable to attend an event for any 
						reason, they may ask the co-author or someone else for 
						help and do the presentation for the registrant. But the 
						registrant should send e-mail for both conference side 
						and the person who will attend.
Written requests for cancellations must be sent to the 
						Conference Secretary. The time would be counted by the 
						refund requests being sent by registrant.
Three Months before conference date: 30USD handing fee 
						would be charged
Two Months before conference date: Refundment of 70% for 
						the registration fee you paid
One Month before conference date: Refundment of 50% for 
						the registration fee you paid
Less Than One Month before conference date: NO REFUND.
Personal Reason
No refund or compensation on "personal reason" for the 
						reason of no attending to the conference. Such as travel 
						difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial 
						default etc.
Force Majeure
No refund or pay any compensation on "force majeure" for 
						the reason of no attending to the conference.
Circumstances amounting to "force majeure" include any 
						event which we could not, even with all due care, 
						foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, 
						flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, 
						break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, 
						industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, 
						adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual 
						or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all 
						similar situations beyond our control.

Link: Visa

Visa Information
With regards to visa inquiries, you can contact âForeign Residents Support Center (FRESC) MOFA Visa Informationâ or âthe Japan Visa Information Hotlineâ listed on the website of
the Embassy or Consulate General of Japan
. Due to the complex nature of visa requirements, we are UNABLE to respond to visa-related inquiries made through this website at the moment. We advise you to contact the Consular Section of the Embassy or Consulate General of Japan nearest you for more information and advice.

If you are a foreign national, please click the links below for specific information related to respective subjects. Please get more details here
https://www.mofa.go.jp/j_info/visit/visa/index.html
Invitation Letters
1. The invitation letter will be issued after the successful registration.
2. Your name must be listed exactly as it appears on your passport. Any differences between the name on your passport and the name on your invitation letter or other documentation could lead to a delay and/or denial of your visa.
3. Please note ICFCC Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference are not authorized to assist with the VISA process beyond providing the Notification of Invitation Letter issued by 
						ICFCC Conferences and conference Committee Board. Should your application be denied, 
						ICFCC Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference cannot change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, nor will 
						ICFCC Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference engage in discussion or correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot be refunded when the VISA application of individual being denied.
4. It takes around one month for the Embassy to process the VISA application, please register as early as you can to make sure you have enough time.

=== PAGE: https://icfcc.org/presentation.html ===
Link: Presentations
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Presentations and Equipment
All presentation rooms are equipped with a screen, an 
						LCD projector, and a laptop computer installed with 
						PowerPoint software. You will be able to insert your USB 
						flash drive into the computer and double click on your 
						presentation to open it in PowerPoint. We recommend that 
						you bring two copies of your presentation in case of one 
						fails. You may also link your own laptop computer to the 
						projector cable, however if you use your own Mac please 
						ensure you have the requisite connector.
Session Chairs
Session Chairs are asked to introduce themselves and 
						other speakers (briefly), and ensure that the session 
						begins and ends on time, and that the time is divided 
						fairly between the presentations. Each presenter should 
						have about 15 minutes in which to present his or her 
						paper, and respond to any questions.
A Polite Request to All Participants
Participants are requested to arrive in a timely fashion 
						for all addresses, whether to their own, or to those of 
						other presenters. Presenters are reminded that the time 
						slots should be divided fairly and equally between the 
						number of presentations, and that they should not 
						overrun. The session chair is asked to assume this 
						timekeeping role.

ICFCC 2025
, June 
						28-30, 2025 |  Jeju Island, South Korea
The 17th International Conference on Future Computer and 
						Communication (ICFCC 2025) was held in Jeju Island, South Korea during June 28 to 30, 2024. The conference is 
						sponsored by Science and Engineering Institute (SCIEI), 
						Sensors and Systems Society of Singapore (SSS), 
						supported by Okayama University, University of Ãvora, 
						Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University, and National Taiwan 
						University of Science and Technology.
Enjoy the Conference
ICFCC
2025 is a great chance for sharing the latest insights of academic and industrial research, bringing together interested academics and industry experts. Prof. Zuqing Zhu, University of Science and Technology of China; Prof. Hung-Hsu Tsai, National Chung Hsing 
						University; Prof. Emanuel S. Grant, University of North 
						Dakota make their excellent speeches.;
Then parallel sessions was held until, and main discussed about Design and Development of Image-based Information Systems; Model Design, Algorithm Optimization, and System Evaluation in Advanced Electronics and Information Systems; Modern Information Networks and Security; Modern Power Systems and Energy Engineering; Software and Mobile Information System Design; Data Based Computational Models and Information Management;
Data based image models and detection techniques. Around 80 people join this event, 6 onsite sessions and 3 online sessions were divided. One best presentation will be selected from each session, evaluated from: originality; applicability; technical Merit; qualities of PPT; English. 9 best presenters were selected.

Interested Listeners & Groups & Sponsors, please contact

icfcc@sciei.org
for details.
Submission Method
The online submission system is opened here:
https://www.zmeeting.org/submission/icfcc2026
You need an account, if you don't have, you can register 
						first.
Submission Guidelines
â¢ Abstracts or full papers will be accepted in English 
						only.
â¢ An international panel of reviewers will review all 
						abstracts or papers anonymously.
â¢ Prospective authors are invited to submit original, 
						high quality papers which is original and has not been 
						submitted and published elsewhere.
â¢ There is no fee for submitting an abstract.
â¢ The title must not exceed 125 characters in length; 
						this includes spaces.
â¢ Manuscripts should be at least 5 full pages and at 
						most 10 pages.
â¢ For one author, you can submit at most 5 papers.
â¢ The reviewing process of the ICFCC conference aims to 
						provide authors with constructive feedback on their 
						papers, even when a submission is rejected. All 
						submissions will be subjected to double-blind peer 
						reviews, who are expert or have been experiencing in the 
						related field for years. The accepted papers must be 
						revised, taking into consideration the referees' 
						comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the 
						conference proceedings.
Review Process
We encourage submissions from under-represented areas. 
						We recognize that not every paper will necessarily 
						contain new theoretical results or new algorithms, and 
						strongly encourages the submissions that provide new 
						models and analyses that are rigorously validated 
						through implementation and experimentation. We will make 
						every effort to fairly review papers with a systems 
						focus.
Policy on Multiple Submissions
We will not accept any paper which, at the time of 
						submission, is under review, is accepted for 
						publication, or has already been published in another 
						conference or a journal. ICFCC is utterly intolerant of 
						plagiarism. Submitted papers are expected to contain 
						original work executed by the authors with adequate, 
						proper and scholarly citations to the work of others. It 
						is the job of the authors to clearly identify both their 
						own contribution(s) and also published results / 
						techniques on which they depend or build. Reviewers are 
						charged to ensure these standards are met.
Instructions for Accepted Papers
After registration, the final paper submitted designates 
						your paper as "Camera Ready" and it will be available 
						online as part of the conference proceedings. Please 
						note that there will not be an additional opportunity to 
						revise your paper. In preparing for the proceedings, 
						please follow the author guidelines posted above under 
						"Paper Format."

Link: Conference Terms
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Refund/Cancellation Policy
If a registrant is unable to attend an event for any 
						reason, they may ask the co-author or someone else for 
						help and do the presentation for the registrant. But the 
						registrant should send e-mail for both conference side 
						and the person who will attend.
Written requests for cancellations must be sent to the 
						Conference Secretary. The time would be counted by the 
						refund requests being sent by registrant.
Three Months before conference date: 30USD handing fee 
						would be charged
Two Months before conference date: Refundment of 70% for 
						the registration fee you paid
One Month before conference date: Refundment of 50% for 
						the registration fee you paid
Less Than One Month before conference date: NO REFUND.
Personal Reason
No refund or compensation on "personal reason" for the 
						reason of no attending to the conference. Such as travel 
						difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial 
						default etc.
Force Majeure
No refund or pay any compensation on "force majeure" for 
						the reason of no attending to the conference.
Circumstances amounting to "force majeure" include any 
						event which we could not, even with all due care, 
						foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, 
						flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, 
						break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, 
						industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, 
						adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual 
						or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all 
						similar situations beyond our control.

Link: Presentations
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Presentations and Equipment
All presentation rooms are equipped with a screen, an 
						LCD projector, and a laptop computer installed with 
						PowerPoint software. You will be able to insert your USB 
						flash drive into the computer and double click on your 
						presentation to open it in PowerPoint. We recommend that 
						you bring two copies of your presentation in case of one 
						fails. You may also link your own laptop computer to the 
						projector cable, however if you use your own Mac please 
						ensure you have the requisite connector.
Session Chairs
Session Chairs are asked to introduce themselves and 
						other speakers (briefly), and ensure that the session 
						begins and ends on time, and that the time is divided 
						fairly between the presentations. Each presenter should 
						have about 15 minutes in which to present his or her 
						paper, and respond to any questions.
A Polite Request to All Participants
Participants are requested to arrive in a timely fashion 
						for all addresses, whether to their own, or to those of 
						other presenters. Presenters are reminded that the time 
						slots should be divided fairly and equally between the 
						number of presentations, and that they should not 
						overrun. The session chair is asked to assume this 
						timekeeping role.

Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Call for Special Sessions
In addition to the technical sessions, keynote addresses 
						etc., ICFCC 202
6
will held a series of half day special 
						sessions. ICFCC welcome the proposal of special 
						sessions.
These special sessions should highlight current topics 
						related to computer and communication, and should include regular 
						papers, invited presentations, and panels that encourage 
						the participation of attendees in active discussion. We 
						intend to organize a fruitful exchange of opinions and 
						ideas between specialists in power engineering, as well 
						as encourage and facilitate interdisciplinary 
						communication amongst university researches and industry 
						professionals involved in power engineering.
Special Session Proposal
Special-session proposals should be submitted by the 
						prospective organizer(s) who will commit to promoting 
						and handling the review process of their special session 
						as Chairs or Co-Chairs of the event. Proposals should 
						include the following information:
â¢ Title of the Special Session

						contact person, biodata)
â¢ Brief description of special session

						published and included in program only.
â¢ Listeners & Groups & Sponsors
Interested Listeners & Groups & Sponsors, please contact 
						with
icfcc@sciei.org
for details.
Submission Method
The online submission system is opened here:
https://www.zmeeting.org/submission/icfcc2026
You need an account, if you don't have, you can register 
						first.
Submission Guidelines
â¢ Abstracts or full papers will be accepted in English 
						only.
â¢ An international panel of reviewers will review all 
						abstracts or papers anonymously.
â¢ Prospective authors are invited to submit original, 
						high quality papers which is original and has not been 
						submitted and published elsewhere.
â¢ There is no fee for submitting an abstract.
â¢ The title must not exceed 125 characters in length; 
						this includes spaces.
â¢ Manuscripts should be at least 5 full pages and at 
						most 10 pages.
â¢ For one author, you can submit at most 5 papers.
â¢ The reviewing process of the ICFCC conference aims to 
						provide authors with constructive feedback on their 
						papers, even when a submission is rejected. All 
						submissions will be subjected to double-blind peer 
						reviews, who are expert or have been experiencing in the 
						related field for years. The accepted papers must be 
						revised, taking into consideration the referees' 
						comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the 
						conference proceedings.
Review Process
We encourage submissions from under-represented areas. 
						We recognize that not every paper will necessarily 
						contain new theoretical results or new algorithms, and 
						strongly encourages the submissions that provide new 
						models and analyses that are rigorously validated 
						through implementation and experimentation. We will make 
						every effort to fairly review papers with a systems 
						focus.
Policy on Multiple Submissions
We will not accept any paper which, at the time of 
						submission, is under review, is accepted for 
						publication, or has already been published in another 
						conference or a journal. ICFCC is utterly intolerant of 
						plagiarism. Submitted papers are expected to contain 
						original work executed by the authors with adequate, 
						proper and scholarly citations to the work of others. It 
						is the job of the authors to clearly identify both their 
						own contribution(s) and also published results / 
						techniques on which they depend or build. Reviewers are 
						charged to ensure these standards are met.
Instructions for Accepted Papers
After registration, the final paper submitted designates 
						your paper as "Camera Ready" and it will be available 
						online as part of the conference proceedings. Please 
						note that there will not be an additional opportunity to 
						revise your paper. In preparing for the proceedings, 
						please follow the author guidelines posted above under 
						"Paper Format."

Link: Presentations
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Presentations and Equipment
All presentation rooms are equipped with a screen, an 
						LCD projector, and a laptop computer installed with 
						PowerPoint software. You will be able to insert your USB 
						flash drive into the computer and double click on your 
						presentation to open it in PowerPoint. We recommend that 
						you bring two copies of your presentation in case of one 
						fails. You may also link your own laptop computer to the 
						projector cable, however if you use your own Mac please 
						ensure you have the requisite connector.
Session Chairs
Session Chairs are asked to introduce themselves and 
						other speakers (briefly), and ensure that the session 
						begins and ends on time, and that the time is divided 
						fairly between the presentations. Each presenter should 
						have about 15 minutes in which to present his or her 
						paper, and respond to any questions.
A Polite Request to All Participants
Participants are requested to arrive in a timely fashion 
						for all addresses, whether to their own, or to those of 
						other presenters. Presenters are reminded that the time 
						slots should be divided fairly and equally between the 
						number of presentations, and that they should not 
						overrun. The session chair is asked to assume this 
						timekeeping role.

Link: Conference Terms
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Refund/Cancellation Policy
If a registrant is unable to attend an event for any 
						reason, they may ask the co-author or someone else for 
						help and do the presentation for the registrant. But the 
						registrant should send e-mail for both conference side 
						and the person who will attend.
Written requests for cancellations must be sent to the 
						Conference Secretary. The time would be counted by the 
						refund requests being sent by registrant.
Three Months before conference date: 30USD handing fee 
						would be charged
Two Months before conference date: Refundment of 70% for 
						the registration fee you paid
One Month before conference date: Refundment of 50% for 
						the registration fee you paid
Less Than One Month before conference date: NO REFUND.
Personal Reason
No refund or compensation on "personal reason" for the 
						reason of no attending to the conference. Such as travel 
						difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial 
						default etc.
Force Majeure
No refund or pay any compensation on "force majeure" for 
						the reason of no attending to the conference.
Circumstances amounting to "force majeure" include any 
						event which we could not, even with all due care, 
						foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, 
						flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, 
						break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, 
						industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, 
						adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual 
						or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all 
						similar situations beyond our control.

ICFCC 2025
, June 
						28-30, 2025 |  Jeju Island, South Korea
The 17th International Conference on Future Computer and 
						Communication (ICFCC 2025) was held in Jeju Island, South Korea during June 28 to 30, 2024. The conference is 
						sponsored by Science and Engineering Institute (SCIEI), 
						Sensors and Systems Society of Singapore (SSS), 
						supported by Okayama University, University of Ãvora, 
						Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University, and National Taiwan 
						University of Science and Technology.
Enjoy the Conference
ICFCC
2025 is a great chance for sharing the latest insights of academic and industrial research, bringing together interested academics and industry experts. Prof. Zuqing Zhu, University of Science and Technology of China; Prof. Hung-Hsu Tsai, National Chung Hsing 
						University; Prof. Emanuel S. Grant, University of North 
						Dakota make their excellent speeches.;
Then parallel sessions was held until, and main discussed about Design and Development of Image-based Information Systems; Model Design, Algorithm Optimization, and System Evaluation in Advanced Electronics and Information Systems; Modern Information Networks and Security; Modern Power Systems and Energy Engineering; Software and Mobile Information System Design; Data Based Computational Models and Information Management;
Data based image models and detection techniques. Around 80 people join this event, 6 onsite sessions and 3 online sessions were divided. One best presentation will be selected from each session, evaluated from: originality; applicability; technical Merit; qualities of PPT; English. 9 best presenters were selected.

18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Keynote Speaker I
Prof. Qingzu Zhu
University of Science and Technology of China, China
Speech Title: TBA
Abstract:
TBA
Biography:
Zuqing Zhu 
						received his Ph.D. degree from the Department of 
						Electrical and Computer Engineering, University of 
						California, Davis, in 2007. From 2007 to 2011, he worked 
						in the Service Provider Technology Group of Cisco 
						Systems, San Jose, California, as a Senior Engineer. In 
						January 2011, he joined the University of Science and 
						Technology of China, where he currently is a Full 
						Professor in the School of Information Science and 
						Technology. He has published 360+ papers in 
						peer-reviewed journals and conferences. He is the 
						Steering Committee Chair of the IEEE International 
						Conference on High Performance Switching and Routing 
						(HPSR), and was the Chair of the Technical Committee on 
						Optical Networking (ONTC) in IEEE Communications 
						Society. He has received the Best Paper Awards from ICC 
						2013, GLOBECOM 2013, ICNC 2014, ICC 2015, ONDM 2018, and 
						GLOBECOM 2023. He is a Fellow of IEEE.
Speakers in 2026 to be announced soon......

Link: Special Sessions
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Call for Special Sessions
In addition to the technical sessions, keynote addresses 
						etc., ICFCC 202
6
will held a series of half day special 
						sessions. ICFCC welcome the proposal of special 
						sessions.
These special sessions should highlight current topics 
						related to computer and communication, and should include regular 
						papers, invited presentations, and panels that encourage 
						the participation of attendees in active discussion. We 
						intend to organize a fruitful exchange of opinions and 
						ideas between specialists in power engineering, as well 
						as encourage and facilitate interdisciplinary 
						communication amongst university researches and industry 
						professionals involved in power engineering.
Special Session Proposal
Special-session proposals should be submitted by the 
						prospective organizer(s) who will commit to promoting 
						and handling the review process of their special session 
						as Chairs or Co-Chairs of the event. Proposals should 
						include the following information:
â¢ Title of the Special Session
â¢ General information of organizers (mail address of main 
						contact person, biodata)
â¢ Brief description of special session
â¢ Scope and topics of of the special session
â¢ Names of potential participants, such as program 
						committee members and invited speakers

â¢ Listeners & Groups & Sponsors
Interested Listeners & Groups & Sponsors, please contact 
						with
icfcc@sciei.org
for details.
Submission Method
The online submission system is opened here:
https://www.zmeeting.org/submission/icfcc2026
You need an account, if you don't have, you can register 
						first.
Submission Guidelines
â¢ Abstracts or full papers will be accepted in English 
						only.
â¢ An international panel of reviewers will review all 
						abstracts or papers anonymously.
â¢ Prospective authors are invited to submit original, 
						high quality papers which is original and has not been 
						submitted and published elsewhere.
â¢ There is no fee for submitting an abstract.
â¢ The title must not exceed 125 characters in length; 
						this includes spaces.
â¢ Manuscripts should be at least 5 full pages and at 
						most 10 pages.
â¢ For one author, you can submit at most 5 papers.
â¢ The reviewing process of the ICFCC conference aims to 
						provide authors with constructive feedback on their 
						papers, even when a submission is rejected. All 
						submissions will be subjected to double-blind peer 
						reviews, who are expert or have been experiencing in the 
						related field for years. The accepted papers must be 
						revised, taking into consideration the referees' 
						comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the 
						conference proceedings.
Review Process
We encourage submissions from under-represented areas. 
						We recognize that not every paper will necessarily 
						contain new theoretical results or new algorithms, and 
						strongly encourages the submissions that provide new 
						models and analyses that are rigorously validated 
						through implementation and experimentation. We will make 
						every effort to fairly review papers with a systems 
						focus.
Policy on Multiple Submissions
We will not accept any paper which, at the time of 
						submission, is under review, is accepted for 
						publication, or has already been published in another 
						conference or a journal. ICFCC is utterly intolerant of 
						plagiarism. Submitted papers are expected to contain 
						original work executed by the authors with adequate, 
						proper and scholarly citations to the work of others. It 
						is the job of the authors to clearly identify both their 
						own contribution(s) and also published results / 
						techniques on which they depend or build. Reviewers are 
						charged to ensure these standards are met.
Instructions for Accepted Papers
After registration, the final paper submitted designates 
						your paper as "Camera Ready" and it will be available 
						online as part of the conference proceedings. Please 
						note that there will not be an additional opportunity to 
						revise your paper. In preparing for the proceedings, 
						please follow the author guidelines posted above under 
						"Paper Format."

Link: Conference Terms
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Refund/Cancellation Policy
If a registrant is unable to attend an event for any 
						reason, they may ask the co-author or someone else for 
						help and do the presentation for the registrant. But the 
						registrant should send e-mail for both conference side 
						and the person who will attend.
Written requests for cancellations must be sent to the 
						Conference Secretary. The time would be counted by the 
						refund requests being sent by registrant.
Three Months before conference date: 30USD handing fee 
						would be charged
Two Months before conference date: Refundment of 70% for 
						the registration fee you paid
One Month before conference date: Refundment of 50% for 
						the registration fee you paid
Less Than One Month before conference date: NO REFUND.
Personal Reason
No refund or compensation on "personal reason" for the 
						reason of no attending to the conference. Such as travel 
						difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial 
						default etc.
Force Majeure
No refund or pay any compensation on "force majeure" for 
						the reason of no attending to the conference.
Circumstances amounting to "force majeure" include any 
						event which we could not, even with all due care, 
						foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, 
						flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, 
						break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, 
						industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, 
						adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual 
						or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all 
						similar situations beyond our control.

Visa Information
With regards to visa inquiries, you can contact âForeign Residents Support Center (FRESC) MOFA Visa Informationâ or âthe Japan Visa Information Hotlineâ listed on the website of
the Embassy or Consulate General of Japan

Invitation Letters
1. The invitation letter will be issued after the successful registration.
2. Your name must be listed exactly as it appears on your passport. Any differences between the name on your passport and the name on your invitation letter or other documentation could lead to a delay and/or denial of your visa.

						ICFCC Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference engage in discussion or correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot be refunded when the VISA application of individual being denied.

=== PAGE: https://icfcc.org/presentation.html ===
Link: Presentations
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Presentations and Equipment
All presentation rooms are equipped with a screen, an 
						LCD projector, and a laptop computer installed with 
						PowerPoint software. You will be able to insert your USB 
						flash drive into the computer and double click on your 
						presentation to open it in PowerPoint. We recommend that 
						you bring two copies of your presentation in case of one 
						fails. You may also link your own laptop computer to the 
						projector cable, however if you use your own Mac please 
						ensure you have the requisite connector.
Session Chairs
Session Chairs are asked to introduce themselves and 
						other speakers (briefly), and ensure that the session 
						begins and ends on time, and that the time is divided 
						fairly between the presentations. Each presenter should 
						have about 15 minutes in which to present his or her 
						paper, and respond to any questions.
A Polite Request to All Participants
Participants are requested to arrive in a timely fashion 
						for all addresses, whether to their own, or to those of 
						other presenters. Presenters are reminded that the time 
						slots should be divided fairly and equally between the 
						number of presentations, and that they should not 
						overrun. The session chair is asked to assume this 
						timekeeping role.

18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Keynote Speaker I
Prof. Qingzu Zhu
University of Science and Technology of China, China
Speech Title: TBA
Abstract:
TBA
Biography:
Zuqing Zhu 
						received his Ph.D. degree from the Department of 
						Electrical and Computer Engineering, University of 
						California, Davis, in 2007. From 2007 to 2011, he worked 
						in the Service Provider Technology Group of Cisco 
						Systems, San Jose, California, as a Senior Engineer. In 
						January 2011, he joined the University of Science and 
						Technology of China, where he currently is a Full 
						Professor in the School of Information Science and 
						Technology. He has published 360+ papers in 
						peer-reviewed journals and conferences. He is the 
						Steering Committee Chair of the IEEE International 
						Conference on High Performance Switching and Routing 
						(HPSR), and was the Chair of the Technical Committee on 
						Optical Networking (ONTC) in IEEE Communications 
						Society. He has received the Best Paper Awards from ICC 
						2013, GLOBECOM 2013, ICNC 2014, ICC 2015, ONDM 2018, and 
						GLOBECOM 2023. He is a Fellow of IEEE.
Speakers in 2026 to be announced soon......

Link: Special Sessions
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Call for Special Sessions
In addition to the technical sessions, keynote addresses 
						etc., ICFCC 202
6
will held a series of half day special 
						sessions. ICFCC welcome the proposal of special 
						sessions.
These special sessions should highlight current topics 
						related to computer and communication, and should include regular 
						papers, invited presentations, and panels that encourage 
						the participation of attendees in active discussion. We 
						intend to organize a fruitful exchange of opinions and 
						ideas between specialists in power engineering, as well 
						as encourage and facilitate interdisciplinary 
						communication amongst university researches and industry 
						professionals involved in power engineering.
Special Session Proposal
Special-session proposals should be submitted by the 
						prospective organizer(s) who will commit to promoting 
						and handling the review process of their special session 
						as Chairs or Co-Chairs of the event. Proposals should 
						include the following information:
â¢ Title of the Special Session
â¢ General information of organizers (mail address of main 
						contact person, biodata)
â¢ Brief description of special session
â¢ Scope and topics of of the special session
â¢ Names of potential participants, such as program 
						committee members and invited speakers
â¢ Please send your proposal to
icfcc@sciei.org
.
Key Day of Special Session Proposal

						April 10, 202
6

6
â¢ Complete set: April 20, 202
6

=== PAGE: https://icfcc.org/visa.html ===
Link: Visa
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Visa Information
With regards to visa inquiries, you can contact âForeign Residents Support Center (FRESC) MOFA Visa Informationâ or âthe Japan Visa Information Hotlineâ listed on the website of
the Embassy or Consulate General of Japan
. Due to the complex nature of visa requirements, we are UNABLE to respond to visa-related inquiries made through this website at the moment. We advise you to contact the Consular Section of the Embassy or Consulate General of Japan nearest you for more information and advice.

If you are a foreign national, please click the links below for specific information related to respective subjects. Please get more details here
https://www.mofa.go.jp/j_info/visit/visa/index.html
Invitation Letters
1. The invitation letter will be issued after the successful registration.
2. Your name must be listed exactly as it appears on your passport. Any differences between the name on your passport and the name on your invitation letter or other documentation could lead to a delay and/or denial of your visa.
3. Please note ICFCC Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference are not authorized to assist with the VISA process beyond providing the Notification of Invitation Letter issued by 
						ICFCC Conferences and conference Committee Board. Should your application be denied, 
						ICFCC Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference cannot change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, nor will 
						ICFCC Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference engage in discussion or correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot be refunded when the VISA application of individual being denied.
4. It takes around one month for the Embassy to process the VISA application, please register as early as you can to make sure you have enough time.

=== PAGE: https://icfcc.org/presentation.html ===
Link: Presentations
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Presentations and Equipment
All presentation rooms are equipped with a screen, an 
						LCD projector, and a laptop computer installed with 
						PowerPoint software. You will be able to insert your USB 
						flash drive into the computer and double click on your 
						presentation to open it in PowerPoint. We recommend that 
						you bring two copies of your presentation in case of one 
						fails. You may also link your own laptop computer to the 
						projector cable, however if you use your own Mac please 
						ensure you have the requisite connector.
Session Chairs
Session Chairs are asked to introduce themselves and 
						other speakers (briefly), and ensure that the session 
						begins and ends on time, and that the time is divided 
						fairly between the presentations. Each presenter should 
						have about 15 minutes in which to present his or her 
						paper, and respond to any questions.
A Polite Request to All Participants
Participants are requested to arrive in a timely fashion 
						for all addresses, whether to their own, or to those of 
						other presenters. Presenters are reminded that the time 
						slots should be divided fairly and equally between the 
						number of presentations, and that they should not 
						overrun. The session chair is asked to assume this 
						timekeeping role.

=== PAGE: https://icfcc.org/2025.html ===
Link: ICFCC 2025
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
ICFCC 2025
, June 
						28-30, 2025 |  Jeju Island, South Korea
The 17th International Conference on Future Computer and 
						Communication (ICFCC 2025) was held in Jeju Island, South Korea during June 28 to 30, 2024. The conference is 
						sponsored by Science and Engineering Institute (SCIEI), 
						Sensors and Systems Society of Singapore (SSS), 
						supported by Okayama University, University of Ãvora, 
						Gifu Shotoku Gakuen University, and National Taiwan 
						University of Science and Technology.
Enjoy the Conference
ICFCC
2025 is a great chance for sharing the latest insights of academic and industrial research, bringing together interested academics and industry experts. Prof. Zuqing Zhu, University of Science and Technology of China; Prof. Hung-Hsu Tsai, National Chung Hsing 
						University; Prof. Emanuel S. Grant, University of North 
						Dakota make their excellent speeches.;
Then parallel sessions was held until, and main discussed about Design and Development of Image-based Information Systems; Model Design, Algorithm Optimization, and System Evaluation in Advanced Electronics and Information Systems; Modern Information Networks and Security; Modern Power Systems and Energy Engineering; Software and Mobile Information System Design; Data Based Computational Models and Information Management;
Data based image models and detection techniques. Around 80 people join this event, 6 onsite sessions and 3 online sessions were divided. One best presentation will be selected from each session, evaluated from: originality; applicability; technical Merit; qualities of PPT; English. 9 best presenters were selected.

â¢ Listeners & Groups & Sponsors
Interested Listeners & Groups & Sponsors, please contact 
						with
icfcc@sciei.org
for details.
Submission Method
The online submission system is opened here:
https://www.zmeeting.org/submission/icfcc2026
You need an account, if you don't have, you can register 
						first.
Submission Guidelines
â¢ Abstracts or full papers will be accepted in English 
						only.
â¢ An international panel of reviewers will review all 
						abstracts or papers anonymously.
â¢ Prospective authors are invited to submit original, 
						high quality papers which is original and has not been 
						submitted and published elsewhere.
â¢ There is no fee for submitting an abstract.
â¢ The title must not exceed 125 characters in length; 
						this includes spaces.
â¢ Manuscripts should be at least 5 full pages and at 
						most 10 pages.
â¢ For one author, you can submit at most 5 papers.
â¢ The reviewing process of the ICFCC conference aims to 
						provide authors with constructive feedback on their 
						papers, even when a submission is rejected. All 
						submissions will be subjected to double-blind peer 
						reviews, who are expert or have been experiencing in the 
						related field for years. The accepted papers must be 
						revised, taking into consideration the referees' 
						comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the 
						conference proceedings.
Review Process
We encourage submissions from under-represented areas. 
						We recognize that not every paper will necessarily 
						contain new theoretical results or new algorithms, and 
						strongly encourages the submissions that provide new 
						models and analyses that are rigorously validated 
						through implementation and experimentation. We will make 
						every effort to fairly review papers with a systems 
						focus.
Policy on Multiple Submissions
We will not accept any paper which, at the time of 
						submission, is under review, is accepted for 
						publication, or has already been published in another 
						conference or a journal. ICFCC is utterly intolerant of 
						plagiarism. Submitted papers are expected to contain 
						original work executed by the authors with adequate, 
						proper and scholarly citations to the work of others. It 
						is the job of the authors to clearly identify both their 
						own contribution(s) and also published results / 
						techniques on which they depend or build. Reviewers are 
						charged to ensure these standards are met.
Instructions for Accepted Papers
After registration, the final paper submitted designates 
						your paper as "Camera Ready" and it will be available 
						online as part of the conference proceedings. Please 
						note that there will not be an additional opportunity to 
						revise your paper. In preparing for the proceedings, 
						please follow the author guidelines posted above under 
						"Paper Format."

Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication

Refund/Cancellation Policy
If a registrant is unable to attend an event for any 
						reason, they may ask the co-author or someone else for 
						help and do the presentation for the registrant. But the 
						registrant should send e-mail for both conference side 
						and the person who will attend.
Written requests for cancellations must be sent to the 
						Conference Secretary. The time would be counted by the 
						refund requests being sent by registrant.
Three Months before conference date: 30USD handing fee 
						would be charged
Two Months before conference date: Refundment of 70% for 
						the registration fee you paid
One Month before conference date: Refundment of 50% for 
						the registration fee you paid
Less Than One Month before conference date: NO REFUND.
Personal Reason
No refund or compensation on "personal reason" for the 
						reason of no attending to the conference. Such as travel 
						difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial 
						default etc.
Force Majeure
No refund or pay any compensation on "force majeure" for 
						the reason of no attending to the conference.
Circumstances amounting to "force majeure" include any 
						event which we could not, even with all due care, 
						foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, 
						flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, 
						break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, 
						industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, 
						adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual 
						or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all 
						similar situations beyond our control.

Keynote Speaker I
Prof. Qingzu Zhu
University of Science and Technology of China, China
Speech Title: TBA
Abstract:
TBA
Biography:
Zuqing Zhu 
						received his Ph.D. degree from the Department of 
						Electrical and Computer Engineering, University of 
						California, Davis, in 2007. From 2007 to 2011, he worked 
						in the Service Provider Technology Group of Cisco 
						Systems, San Jose, California, as a Senior Engineer. In 
						January 2011, he joined the University of Science and 
						Technology of China, where he currently is a Full 
						Professor in the School of Information Science and 
						Technology. He has published 360+ papers in 
						peer-reviewed journals and conferences. He is the 
						Steering Committee Chair of the IEEE International 
						Conference on High Performance Switching and Routing 
						(HPSR), and was the Chair of the Technical Committee on 
						Optical Networking (ONTC) in IEEE Communications 
						Society. He has received the Best Paper Awards from ICC 
						2013, GLOBECOM 2013, ICNC 2014, ICC 2015, ONDM 2018, and 
						GLOBECOM 2023. He is a Fellow of IEEE.

Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Call for Special Sessions
In addition to the technical sessions, keynote addresses 
						etc., ICFCC 202
6
will held a series of half day special 
						sessions. ICFCC welcome the proposal of special 
						sessions.
These special sessions should highlight current topics 
						related to computer and communication, and should include regular 
						papers, invited presentations, and panels that encourage 
						the participation of attendees in active discussion. We 
						intend to organize a fruitful exchange of opinions and 
						ideas between specialists in power engineering, as well 
						as encourage and facilitate interdisciplinary 
						communication amongst university researches and industry 
						professionals involved in power engineering.
Special Session Proposal
Special-session proposals should be submitted by the 
						prospective organizer(s) who will commit to promoting 
						and handling the review process of their special session 
						as Chairs or Co-Chairs of the event. Proposals should 
						include the following information:
â¢ Title of the Special Session
â¢ General information of organizers (mail address of main 
						contact person, biodata)
â¢ Brief description of special session
â¢ Scope and topics of of the special session
â¢ Names of potential participants, such as program 
						committee members and invited speakers

Link: Submit Now
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Submission Type
â¢ Full Paper (Presentation and Publication)
(
Template Download for Proceedings
) (

)
Accepted full papers will be invited to give

â¢ Abstract (Presentation only)
(
Template Download
)
Accepted abstract will be invited to give presentation 
						at the conference. The presentation will not be 
						published and included in program only.
â¢ Listeners & Groups & Sponsors
Interested Listeners & Groups & Sponsors, please contact 
						with
icfcc@sciei.org
for details.
Submission Method
The online submission system is opened here:
https://www.zmeeting.org/submission/icfcc2026
You need an account, if you don't have, you can register 
						first.
Submission Guidelines
â¢ Abstracts or full papers will be accepted in English 
						only.
â¢ An international panel of reviewers will review all 
						abstracts or papers anonymously.
â¢ Prospective authors are invited to submit original, 
						high quality papers which is original and has not been 
						submitted and published elsewhere.
â¢ There is no fee for submitting an abstract.
â¢ The title must not exceed 125 characters in length; 
						this includes spaces.
â¢ Manuscripts should be at least 5 full pages and at 
						most 10 pages.
â¢ For one author, you can submit at most 5 papers.
â¢ The reviewing process of the ICFCC conference aims to 
						provide authors with constructive feedback on their 
						papers, even when a submission is rejected. All 
						submissions will be subjected to double-blind peer 
						reviews, who are expert or have been experiencing in the 
						related field for years. The accepted papers must be 
						revised, taking into consideration the referees' 
						comments and suggestions, before inclusion in the 
						conference proceedings.
Review Process
We encourage submissions from under-represented areas. 
						We recognize that not every paper will necessarily 
						contain new theoretical results or new algorithms, and 
						strongly encourages the submissions that provide new 
						models and analyses that are rigorously validated 
						through implementation and experimentation. We will make 
						every effort to fairly review papers with a systems 
						focus.
Policy on Multiple Submissions
We will not accept any paper which, at the time of 
						submission, is under review, is accepted for 
						publication, or has already been published in another 
						conference or a journal. ICFCC is utterly intolerant of 
						plagiarism. Submitted papers are expected to contain 
						original work executed by the authors with adequate, 
						proper and scholarly citations to the work of others. It 
						is the job of the authors to clearly identify both their 
						own contribution(s) and also published results / 
						techniques on which they depend or build. Reviewers are 
						charged to ensure these standards are met.
Instructions for Accepted Papers
After registration, the final paper submitted designates 
						your paper as "Camera Ready" and it will be available 
						online as part of the conference proceedings. Please 
						note that there will not be an additional opportunity to 
						revise your paper. In preparing for the proceedings, 
						please follow the author guidelines posted above under 
						"Paper Format."

Three Months before conference date: 30USD handing fee 
						would be charged
Two Months before conference date: Refundment of 70% for 
						the registration fee you paid
One Month before conference date: Refundment of 50% for 
						the registration fee you paid
Less Than One Month before conference date: NO REFUND.
Personal Reason
No refund or compensation on "personal reason" for the 
						reason of no attending to the conference. Such as travel 
						difficulties, visa problems, health issues, financial 
						default etc.
Force Majeure
No refund or pay any compensation on "force majeure" for 
						the reason of no attending to the conference.
Circumstances amounting to "force majeure" include any 
						event which we could not, even with all due care, 
						foresee or avoid. Such circumstances include the fire, 
						flood, explosion, storm or other weather damage, 
						break-in, criminal damage, riots or civil strife, 
						industrial action, natural or nuclear disaster, fire, 
						adverse weather conditions, war or threat of war, actual 
						or threatened terrorist activity, epidemic and all 
						similar situations beyond our control.

Link: Visa
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication

Visa Information
With regards to visa inquiries, you can contact âForeign Residents Support Center (FRESC) MOFA Visa Informationâ or âthe Japan Visa Information Hotlineâ listed on the website of
the Embassy or Consulate General of Japan
. Due to the complex nature of visa requirements, we are UNABLE to respond to visa-related inquiries made through this website at the moment. We advise you to contact the Consular Section of the Embassy or Consulate General of Japan nearest you for more information and advice.

If you are a foreign national, please click the links below for specific information related to respective subjects. Please get more details here
https://www.mofa.go.jp/j_info/visit/visa/index.html
Invitation Letters
1. The invitation letter will be issued after the successful registration.
2. Your name must be listed exactly as it appears on your passport. Any differences between the name on your passport and the name on your invitation letter or other documentation could lead to a delay and/or denial of your visa.
3. Please note ICFCC Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference are not authorized to assist with the VISA process beyond providing the Notification of Invitation Letter issued by 
						ICFCC Conferences and conference Committee Board. Should your application be denied, 
						ICFCC Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference cannot change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, nor will 
						ICFCC Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference engage in discussion or correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot be refunded when the VISA application of individual being denied.
4. It takes around one month for the Embassy to process the VISA application, please register as early as you can to make sure you have enough time.

=== PAGE: https://icfcc.org/presentation.html ===
Link: Presentations
Title: 18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
18th ICFCC-Future Computer and Communication
Presentations and Equipment
All presentation rooms are equipped with a screen, an 
						LCD projector, and a laptop computer installed with 
						PowerPoint software. You will be able to insert your USB 
						flash drive into the computer and double click on your 
						presentation to open it in PowerPoint. We recommend that 
						you bring two copies of your presentation in case of one 
						fails. You may also link your own laptop computer to the 
						projector cable, however if you use your own Mac please 
						ensure you have the requisite connector.
Session Chairs
Session Chairs are asked to introduce themselves and 
						other speakers (briefly), and ensure that the session 
						begins and ends on time, and that the time is divided 
						fairly between the presentations. Each presenter should 
						have about 15 minutes in which to present his or her 
						paper, and respond to any questions.
A Polite Request to All Participants
Participants are requested to arrive in a timely fashion 
						for all addresses, whether to their own, or to those of 
						other presenters. Presenters are reminded that the time 
						slots should be divided fairly and equally between the 
						number of presentations, and that they should not 
						overrun. The session chair is asked to assume this 
						timekeeping role.
